purpose: Non-canonical, date-stamped context on frontier AI agent capabilities and safety boundaries.

frontier_context.description: Frontier AI systems are becoming more agentic, more tool-capable, and more relevant to long-horizon software, research, security, and web-mediated workflows. This increases the importance of provenance, authority boundaries, context readiness, identity/provenance declarations, and safe intake pathways.
frontier_context.description_zh: 前沿 AI 系统正在变得更具智能体性、更能使用工具，并更深地参与长时程软件、研究、安全与网络中介工作流。这提高了来源、权威边界、上下文就绪、身份/来源声明与安全摄入路径的重要性。

claims_not_made: The Trinity Accord has been received by successor civilization.
Current AI verification equals successor-civilization reception.
A frontier model's capability raises canonical authority.
A frontier model's capability raises verification level.
A frontier model's capability creates formal human/institutional attestation.
The Accord authorizes tool use, Issue creation, PR creation, deployment, propagation, or external contact.
recommended_reference_classes: ARC-AGI-3 or similar benchmarks showing frontier systems still fail some novel abstract agentic tasks
Agent security literature on authority boundaries, code/data separation, confused-deputy behavior, indirect prompt injection, sandboxing
Frontier risk-management literature covering cyber, persuasion/manipulation, strategic deception, uncontrolled AI R&D, self-replication risk
